name: Brihuega, Cívica, and Lavender Fields Loop
name_original: Brihuega - Cívica – circular por los campos de lavanda
description: Cycle 19.6 miles through the Brihuega lavender fields and visit Cívica on this moderate mountain biking route with 1559 feet of gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Brihuega offers diverse terrain within the picturesque Tajuña Valley, characterized by varied topography including hills, valleys, and ancient paths. The region features natural landscapes such as extensive lavender fields and river routes, providing a scenic backdrop for rides. Many trails follow historical routes that once connected villages, offering a blend of outdoor activity and cultural exploration. This area provides a dynamic environment for mountain biking, with routes winding through different elevations and natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Brihuega The most popular mountain bike trail is Brihuega - Lupiana – circular por el río Ungría y Matayeguas, a 42.4 miles (68.3 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation changes as it winds through the Tajuña Valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Brihuega, Cívica, and Lavender Fields Loop, a moderate 19.6 miles (31.6 km) path. This trail offers views of the iconic lavender fields and passes through historical villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Cuevas Bodega loop from Valdesaz, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Brihuega is defined by the Tajuña Valley, extensive lavender fields, and ancient paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Brihuega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Brihuega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Brihuega?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Brihuega, offering a range of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times, highlighting the area's popularity for mountain biking.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Brihuega?
A: The trails in Brihuega wind through diverse terrain within the picturesque Tajuña Valley. You'll encounter varied topography, including hills, valleys, and ancient paths. Some routes feature challenging ascents and exhilarating descents, with technical sections like rock gardens, while others offer more moderate rides through natural landscapes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Brihuega?
A: Yes, while many trails are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available in Brihuega. These routes are generally flatter and shorter, making them ideal for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Brihuega offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005350343/brihuega-lupiana-circular-por-el-rio-ungria-y-matayeguas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega - Lupiana – circular por el río Ungría y Matayeguas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.4-mile trail with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Baja de Torija – Castillo de Torija loop from Aparcamiento de emergencia Vialidad Invernal Trijueque&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 39 miles with substantial climbing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer stunning views of the Tajuña Valley and its exuberant vegetation. Depending on the route, you might pass through the iconic lavender fields, especially vibrant in July, or explore ancient paths connecting villages. You can also find historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arab Caves of Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5854808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torija&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Brihuega?
A: Yes, Brihuega features several circular mountain bike routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004324022/brihuega-civica-and-lavender-fields-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega, Cívica, and Lavender Fields Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 19.6-mile path offering scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas Bodega loop from Valdesaz&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 32.1-mile moderate trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Brihuega?
A: The best time to visit Brihuega for mountain biking is generally during spring and early summer (May to July) or early autumn (September to October). During July, the extensive lavender fields are in full bloom, offering a spectacular purple landscape. These periods typically provide pleasant temperatures for riding, avoiding the intense heat of mid-summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Brihuega?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly detailed, the region does offer easier paths suitable for all skill levels. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, providing a more relaxed experience for families with children. It's advisable to check the difficulty ratings and terrain descriptions for individual routes to ensure they match your family's abilities.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Brihuega?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the scenic backdrop of the Tajuña Valley. The opportunity to ride through ancient paths and past iconic lavender fields is frequently highlighted as a unique aspect of mountain biking in Brihuega.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Brihuega?
A: Brihuega, known for its historic charm, offers various amenities. The historic center features cobbled streets and ancient buildings, where you can find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. Many trails are accessible from or near the town, making it convenient to refuel and relax after your ride.

Q: Are there any specific highlights or points of interest to look out for?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Brihuega is rich in points of interest. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavender Fields and Arab Caves of Brihuega&lt;/a&gt;, visit the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; itself, or discover nearby settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona Village&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5854808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torija&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the area's rich history.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route available in Brihuega?
A: The longest mountain bike route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005350343/brihuega-lupiana-circular-por-el-rio-ungria-y-matayeguas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega - Lupiana – circular por el río Ungría y Matayeguas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 42.4 miles (68.3 km). This difficult trail offers a significant challenge with its length and elevation changes.

This area of Guadalajara is characterized by its lavender fields that bloom from spring to mid-summer, providing a very picturesque landscape and a very pleasant atmosphere.

Cívica does not go unnoticed in the eyes of those who travel the road and soon they see that the rock in front of their eyes has a door, railings and other architectural elements that attract a lot of attention. It is an abandoned town that was carved out of the rock like a fantasy movie and according to studies, its galleries were used since the Middle Ages.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain biking route is approximately 31.5 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 38 minutes to complete. However, allow extra time to enjoy the scenic lavender fields and points of interest along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While suitable for a range of cyclists, it includes some ascents and varied terrain that can be challenging, especially towards the end of the ride. Riders should have a reasonable level of fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a beautiful landscape with extensive lavender fields, dirt tracks, and paths. The soil can be a mix of fine earth ('tierrilla') and stony sections, with some more technical parts. Be prepared for varied surfaces typical of the Alcarria region.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The absolute best time to visit is from late June to late July, when the lavender fields are in full bloom, with peak bloom typically occurring in the central two weeks of July. This offers a unique visual and aromatic experience. Consider riding during sunrise or sunset for spectacular photographic opportunities.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for mountain biking in the La Alcarria region?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for mountain biking on public trails in the La Alcarria region. However, remember that the lavender fields are private property. Please be respectful, stay on designated paths, and do not cut the lavender.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the area, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Be mindful of the high number of bees in the lavender fields during blooming season.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, often starting and ending near Brihuega. You can typically find parking in or around the town of Brihuega, which serves as a convenient access point for the lavender fields and the start of the trail.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning lavender fields, you'll encounter several notable spots. These include the Fountain of the Twelve Spouts, picturesque Views of the lavender fields, and the unique village of Cívica, known for its architecture carved into limestone.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find amenities along the way?
faqs.answer: The historic town of Brihuega offers various amenities, including cafes and restaurants. The trail research also mentions that cyclists can refuel in places like Pajares along the route. It's always wise to carry enough water and snacks for your ride.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of other paths. Approximately 9% of the route follows the Camino de Brihuega, offering a blend of dedicated and shared trails.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, and appropriate mountain biking gear. Sun protection is essential, especially during summer. Consider comfortable footwear with good grip if you plan to dismount and explore.
